description: Announcing the return of a discontinued or seasonal product to the market, emphasizing exclusivity and urgency of limited availability.
structural_recipe: Feature the returning product prominently in visual (hero shot or carousel). Caption should include: (1) excitement marker ('Vuelve', 'De regreso', 'Limited time'), (2) product name and key attribute (flavor, specialty), (3) specific location/availability, (4) urgency clause emphasizing scarcity or time-limit ('no sabemos cuando vuelve', 'mientras duren', 'por tiempo limitado'). Use direct call-to-action ('No puedes perder') to drive store visits.
why_it_works: Saudi consumers respond to scarcity and exclusivity signals—limited re-availability creates FOMO. Nostalgia for discontinued favorites re-engages past customers. Specific location tagging drives foot traffic. Urgency language ('don't know when it returns') compels immediate action rather than delayed consideration.
transplantation_caveats: Verify product actually has historical demand/nostalgia in target market before claiming 'return'
Avoid overstating scarcity if stock is plentiful—erodes trust
Timing matters: launch during peak shopping/eating occasions (Ramadan, weekends, holidays)
Spanish-language caption observed; confirm Arabic/transliteration reaches intended Saudi audience
cultural_constraints: Language: Confirm Spanish captions resonate with Saudi audience or localize to Arabic/English
Timing sensitivity: Align relaunch with local shopping patterns and cultural calendar
